description:
Goal: make compile-time environment a first-class compiler2 value backed by namespace savepoints and projected as Fz-shaped data only at macro/source boundaries.

Signal:
- a `CompileEnv` records current module, function/context, source/span, namespace head, aliases/imports/requires, and visible lexical variables needed for macro expansion
- `__ENV__`, `__MODULE__`, and `__CALLER__` are Fz-value projections of `CompileEnv`, not independent mutable state and not Rust AST payloads
- tests prove alias/import/require/env mutations produce new namespace/env savepoints without copying whole scopes
- quoted identifiers can carry the env/context required by the hygiene pressure test

Change:
- introduce `CompileEnv` near compiler2 source jobs and namespace code
- define projection from `CompileEnv` to process-heap Fz values for compiler variables
- teach source traversal to thread env savepoints through module/function/macro bodies and source forms
- expose env data to compiler services and macro invocation without letting macros mutate world state directly

Why:
- namespace is the durable authority; macro env values are just Fz-shaped data given to user code
- without a single env model, `require`, `__CALLER__`, hygiene, and source-order definitions will drift into separate ad hoc mechanisms
title: Compiler2 compile env: project Namespace into __ENV__/__MODULE__/__CALLER__
